name: Information Security Compliance Policy - Passage 1
text:
XXX-ORG-IMS-POL-021

Abstract

This Compliance Policy defines compliance requirements and responsibilities that sets the tone for <<CLIENT>> to comply with relevant policies, statutory, regulatory, contractual and other business requirements.

**STATEMENT OF CONFIDENTIALITY**

All information in this document is <<CLIENT>> PRIVILEGED and for INTERNAL USE ONLY.

The information in this document may contain <<CLIENT>> proprietary trade secrets and confidential information, the disclosure of which could substantially benefit competitors. As a result, this document should not be disclosed, used or duplicated – in whole or in part without the explicit authorization of <<CLIENT>> management.

The information is intended solely for the use of authorized recipients. If you are not the intended recipient, you are prohibited from reading, using, disseminating, distributing and copying this document.

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Information Security Compliance Policy v2.0 UAE IA Mapping 2_passage_3
name: Information Security Compliance Policy - 1 INTRODUCTION
text:
1 INTRODUCTION

The Compliance Policy ("Policy") facilitates the implementation of the associated controls to ensure <<CLIENT>>; is compliant with applicable national and global laws and regulations, inclusive of but not limited to the UAE Federal Law 45 of 2021, the UAE Information Assurance Regulation, ISO 27001, ISO 27701, ISO 20000, ISO 22301 and the National Cyber Risk Management Framework (NCRMF).
1 INTRODUCTION

The Compliance Policy ("Policy") facilitates the implementation of the associated controls to ensure <<CLIENT>> is compliant with applicable national and global laws and regulations, inclusive of but not limited to the UAE Federal Law 45 of 2021, the UAE Information Assurance Regulation, ISO 27001, ISO 27701, ISO 20000, ISO 22301 and the National Cyber Risk Management Framework (NCRMF).

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Information Security Compliance Policy v2.0 UAE IA Mapping 2_passage_4
name: Information Security Compliance Policy - 2 PURPOSE
text:
2 PURPOSE

The purpose of this Compliance Policy ("Policy") is to define compliance requirements, responsibilities, awareness, and monitoring with respect to Information security and privacy across <<CLIENT>>.
The following are the key objectives of this Policy:
    • Identification of applicable requirements: Establishment of an approach for identification of, and compliance to, applicable legislation and contractual requirements.
    • Protection of data: Protection of corporate and customer Intellectual Property Rights (IPR); Personally, Identifiable Information (PII) and records required under relevant laws and regulations.
2 PURPOSE

The purpose of this Compliance Policy ("Policy") is to define compliance requirements, responsibilities, awareness, and monitoring with respect to Information security and privacy across <<CLIENT>>.

The following are the key objectives of this Policy:

- **Identification of applicable requirements:** Establishment of an approach for identification of, and compliance to, applicable legislation and contractual requirements.
- **Protection of data:** Protection of corporate and customer Intellectual Property Rights (IPR); Personally, Identifiable Information (PII) and records required under relevant laws and regulations.

text:
3 SCOPE

This Policy applies to all employees, contractors and third parties, business processes, information, information assets and <<CLIENT>>'s information and communications technology (ICT) systems.

Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the ‘Policy Exception Management procedure’.

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Information Security Compliance Policy v2.0 UAE IA Mapping 2_passage_6
name: Information Security Compliance Policy - 4 POLICY STATEMENT
text:
4 POLICY STATEMENT

<<CLIENT>> shall develop, implement, and maintain an organization-wide information security compliance Policy to:
    • Implement and operate applicable and relevant security measures and controls to ensure compliance with applicable standards, laws and regulations.
    • Avoid breaches of legal, statutory, regulatory or contractual obligations related to information security, business continuity and privacy.
4 POLICY STATEMENT

<<CLIENT>> shall develop, implement, and maintain an organization-wide information security compliance Policy to:

- Implement and operate applicable and relevant security measures and controls to ensure compliance with applicable standards, laws and regulations.
- Avoid breaches of legal, statutory, regulatory or contractual obligations related to information security, business continuity and privacy.

name: Information Security Compliance Policy - 5.1 Identification of Compliance Requirements
text:
5.1 Identification of Compliance Requirements

<<CLIENT>> shall:

1. Identify, define and establish compliance with all relevant statutory, regulatory, and contractual requirements, including those of intellectual property rights and business continuity, and data privacy.
2. Define and implement security controls and assign responsibilities to meet relevant statutory, regulatory, and contractual requirements.

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Information Security Compliance Policy v2.0 UAE IA Mapping 2_passage_8
name: Information Security Compliance Policy - 5.2 Intellectual Property Rights (IPR)
text:
5.2 Intellectual Property Rights (IPR)

<<CLIENT>> shall: 
5.2.1 Implement appropriate processes, controls, responsibilities and system requirements to ensure compliance with intellectual property rights, including but not limited to:
5.2.1.a Maintaining appropriate asset registers and identify all assets with requirements to protect intellectual property rights.
5.2.1.b Acquiring software only through known and reputable sources to ensure that no intellectual property rights are violated.
5.2.1.c Performing verification to ensure that only authorized software and licensed products are installed.
5.2.1.d Enhancing and maintaining awareness to protect intellectual property rights and intent of disciplinary action against employees who breach them.
5.2.1.e Ensuring that proprietary software products are under a license agreement that specifies license terms and conditions.
5.2.1.f Dealing software products created internally, or licensed/provided by any developer to <<CLIENT>> as <<CLIENT>> proprietary material.
5.2.1.g Operational processes for maintaining proof and evidence of ownership of licenses, master disks, manuals, etc.
5.2 Intellectual Property Rights (IPR)

<<CLIENT>> shall:

5.2.1 Implement appropriate processes, controls, responsibilities and system requirements to ensure compliance with intellectual property rights, including but not limited to:

5.2.1.a Maintaining appropriate asset registers and identify all assets with requirements to protect intellectual property rights.

5.2.1.b Acquiring software only through known and reputable sources to ensure that no intellectual property rights are violated.

5.2.1.c Performing verification to ensure that only authorized software and licensed products are installed.

5.2.1.d Enhancing and maintaining awareness to protect intellectual property rights and intent of disciplinary action against employees who breach them.

5.2.1.e Ensuring that proprietary software products are under a license agreement that specifies license terms and conditions.

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Information Security Compliance Policy v2.0 UAE IA Mapping 2_passage_9
name: Information Security Compliance Policy - 5.3 Protection of Records and Personal Data
text:
5.3 Protection of Records and Personal Data

<<CLIENT>> shall:
5.3.1    Define and categorize records by record type (e.g. financial, database, transaction logs, audit logs, operational procedures) and include details of retention periods and type of storage media.
            5.3.2 Store related cryptographic key material to permit decryption of records for the period the records are retained or archived.
            5.3.3 Consider the potential deterioration of media used for storing digital records when protecting them.
            5.3.4 Develop procedures for ensuring access and availability of data to safeguard against loss due to technology changes in case of long-term storage.
            5.3.5 Collect, process, transmit, store, and/ or delete personal data fairly and lawfully.
            5.3.6 Collect and process personal data only for the intended business purpose. Collected and processed personal data shall be acknowledged by the individual concerned (data subject).
            5.3.7 Keep personal data up to date and factual. Inaccurate or incomplete personal data shall be deleted, corrected, supplemented, or updated.
            5.3.8 Delete personal data that is no longer required for the intended business purpose.
            5.3.9 Implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect personal information.
            5.3.10 Develop processes and procedures to ensure that personal data is protected throughout its lifecycle.
5.3 Protection of Records and Personal Data

<<CLIENT>> shall:

5.3.1    Define and categorize records by record type (e.g. financial, database, transaction logs, audit logs, operational procedures) and include details of retention periods and type of storage media.

1. Store related cryptographic key material to permit decryption of records for the period the records are retained or archived.
2. Consider the potential deterioration of media used for storing digital records when protecting them.
3. Develop procedures for ensuring access and availability of data to safeguard against loss due to technology changes in case of long-term storage.
4. Collect, process, transmit, store, and/ or delete personal data fairly and lawfully.
5. Collect and process personal data only for the intended business purpose. Collected and processed personal data shall be acknowledged by the individual concerned (data subject).
6. Keep personal data up to date and factual. Inaccurate or incomplet

name: Information Security Compliance Policy - 5.4 Compliance with Security Policies and Standards
text:
5.4 Compliance with Security Policies and Standards

<<CLIENT>> shall:
            5.4.1 Conduct compliance assessments periodically with respect to applicable standards, laws and regulations.
            5.4.2 Report compliance results to appropriate personnel and senior management.
            5.4.3 Take necessary corrective actions identified.
            5.4.4 Record, monitor and review corrective actions to ensure compliance is maintained.

5.4 Compliance with Security Policies and Standards

<<CLIENT>> shall:

1. Conduct compliance assessments periodically with respect to applicable standards, laws and regulations.
2. Report compliance results to appropriate personnel and senior management.
3. Take necessary corrective actions identified.
4. Record, monitor and review corrective actions to ensure compliance is maintained.
section: 5.4 Compliance with Security Policies and Standards

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Information Security Compliance Policy v2.0 UAE IA Mapping 2_passage_11
name: Information Security Compliance Policy - 6 ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
text:
6 ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES

Executives & Senior Management
    1. Make available and support the required resources to drive Information Security Compliance.
    2. Where relevant, provide direction to and participate in reviews and oversight of Information Security Compliance.
    3. Review and approve this Policy and all related procedures prepared by the <<CLIENT>> Information Security team.
    4. Review and approve the process developed by Information Security to identify ongoing compliance requirements applicable to the organization.
    5. Initiate independent reviews (Internal Audit and/or External Audit) of information security at planned intervals or when a significant change occurs.
IS (Information Security) Team
    1. Determine specific system requirements resulting from the identified compliance requirements.   
    2. Define specific controls to ensure:
        2.1 All intellectual property right protection requirements are met.   
        2.2 All privacy and protection of personally identifiable information requirements are met.   
        2.3 All record protection requirements are met for records with specific compliance requirements.   
        2.4    All cryptographic control requirements are met.
    3 Define and execute a process for routinely checking for technical compliance with security standards.   
    4 Conduct vulnerability assessments to determine compliance.
    5 Ensure compliance checking results are performed by, and the results are reviewed by, authorized personnel with adequate technical capabilities.   
    6 Ensure that external member’s provisions are accounted for in the organization's information systems audit plan and tools.   
    7 Develop a process to identify all applicable requirements organization must meet in terms of protecting intellectual property rights.   
    8 Report any issues detected during technical compliance checking to the appropriate authority for remediation.   
IT (Information Technology) Team
    1. Apply specific controls to ensure all compliance requirements are met.   
    2. Apply specific controls to ensure all cryptographic control requirements are met.
    3. Take corrective action when an independent review identifies that the organization's approach and implementation to managing information security is inadequate.

Human Capital
    1. Assist IS team to determine specific system requirements resulting from the identified (privacy) requirements. 
    2. Ensure the individuals carrying out independent reviews have the appropriate skills and experience to conduct the review.   
Employees and Third Parties (Contractors, Vendors, etc.)
    1. Employees and third parties (including contractors, vendors, etc.) who access organizational premises, information and information assets or use information and communications technology (ICT) systems of the organization are responsible for reporting any compliance violation with respect to this Policy and all its applicable procedures.

Legal
    1. Define a process for identifying data with specific compliance requirements regarding privacy and protection of personally identifiable information.
    2. Identify all relevant and applicable laws and regulations to <<CLIENT>> and communicate to the respective parties.
    3. Define and liaise with IS Team on standard legal clauses and terms for the contract/agreement to be signed with external parties, employment contracts, confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDAs).
    4. Review third-party vendor, clients and partner contracts and involve IS Team for review of clauses concerning Information Security responsibilities.


6 ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES

**Executives & Senior Management**

1. Make available and support the required resources to drive Information Security Compliance.
2. Where relevant, provide direction to and participate in reviews and oversight of Information Security Compliance.
3. Review and approve this Policy and all related procedures prepared by the <<CLIENT>> Information Security team.
4. Review and approve the process developed by Information Security to identify ongoing compliance requirements applicable to the organization.
5. Initiate independent reviews (Internal Audit and/or External Audit) of information security at planned intervals or when a significant change occurs.

**IS (Information Security) Team**

1. Determine specific system requirements resulting from the identified compliance requirements.
2. Define specific controls to ensure:
1. All privacy and protection of personally identifiable information requirements are met.
2. All record protection requi

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Information Security Compliance Policy v2.0 UAE IA Mapping 2_passage_12
name: Information Security Compliance Policy - 7 POLICY ENFORCEMENT AND EXCEPTIONS
text:
7 POLICY ENFORCEMENT AND EXCEPTIONS

All employees, contractors and third parties are responsible for understanding and adhering to this Policy. Any violation of this Policy shall be reported to Policy Owner. Violation of this Policy shall be subjected to disciplinary action by the <<CLIENT>>'s Human Capital Department, taking into consideration the recommendation of IS Team and in accordance with other applicable policies, laws and regulations.
Disciplinary action may range from a verbal warning to discontinuation of access to facility or services, or termination of employment/contract/agreement, and possible legal action. The actual action taken shall depend on factors such as the seriousness of the breach and the user's disciplinary record.
Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the Policy Exception Management procedure.
7 POLICY ENFORCEMENT AND EXCEPTIONS

All employees, contractors and third parties are responsible for understanding and adhering to this Policy. Any violation of this Policy shall be reported to Policy Owner. Violation of this Policy shall be subjected to disciplinary action by the <<CLIENT>>'s Human Capital Department, taking into consideration the recommendation of IS Team and in accordance with other applicable policies, laws and regulations.

Disciplinary action may range from a verbal warning to discontinuation of access to facility or services, or termination of employment/contract/agreement, and possible legal action. The actual action taken shall depend on factors such as the seriousness of the breach and the user's disciplinary record.

Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the Policy Exception Management procedure.

name: Information Security Compliance Policy - 8 MONITORING AND REVIEW
text:
8 MONITORING AND REVIEW

This Policy is developed by <<CLIENT>> Information Security (IS) Team, owned and reviewed by the CISO. It is approved by the CEO. 
<<CLIENT>> IS Team shall initiate a review of this Policy annually or whenever a significant change occurs in the organization's operating environment.
Feedback, suggestions or questions regarding the Policy shall be directed to the <<CLIENT>> IS Team at <<Client ID>>
